channel.title: Stewart Masters
channel.description: Operator perspectives on AI adoption, digital operations, and product execution inside real businesses.

item.title: The AI Problem Is Adoption, Not Capability
item.description: The tools are good enough. The bottleneck is people, process, and organisational readiness. Here's why most AI implementations stall at adoption.

item.title: The Founder's Guide to Stepping Back from Product
item.description: At some point, every founder has to stop being the de facto CPO. Most do it too late and too badly. Here's how to make the transition well.

item.title: Why Most AI Projects Fail in Companies | Stewart Masters
item.description: Most AI projects don't fail because the technology doesn't work. They fail because of decisions made before a single line of code is written.

item.title: How to Evaluate a CTO
item.description: A CTO who built the first version of your product may not be the right person to scale it. Here's how to assess whether you have the right one — and what to do if you don't.

item.title: Why most professionals have no idea what they're worth — or where their money goes
item.description: Most professionals are rigorous with data at work and completely blind about their own finances. Here's why that gap exists and how to close it.

item.title: Why Your AI Strategy Needs a Data Strategy First
item.description: Most AI projects fail not because of the model — but because the data isn't ready. Here's why you need the data strategy before the AI strategy.

item.title: How to Structure a Digital Transformation Budget
item.description: Most digital transformation budgets are built around tools and projects, not outcomes. Here's how to structure one that actually reflects how change happens.

item.title: The NED's Guide to Understanding Technology
item.description: You don't need to be a technologist to be an effective NED on a tech-led business. But you do need to know what questions to ask — and what the answers reveal.

item.title: How to Measure Product-Market Fit Honestly
item.description: Product-market fit is real — but most teams don't know when they have it. Here's how to look past vanity signals and measure it honestly.

item.title: When to Build vs. Buy in a Digital Business
item.description: The build vs. buy decision is never just technical. It's strategic. Here's how to make it without regretting it six months later.

item.title: How Boards Should Evaluate AI Risk
item.description: Boards are being asked to govern AI without the frameworks to do it. Here's a practical approach to evaluating AI risk at board level.

item.title: Data Strategy for Non-Technical Founders
item.description: You don't need to be technical to make good decisions about data. You just need to ask the right questions and avoid the most expensive mistakes.

item.title: Why Most Product Roadmaps Are Fiction
item.description: Roadmaps feel like planning. They're usually something else. Here's why most product roadmaps fail to reflect reality — and what to do instead.

item.title: How to Hire Your First Head of AI
item.description: Most companies hiring for AI leadership are asking the wrong questions. Here's what the role actually requires — and how to find someone who can do it.

item.title: The Real Cost of Tech Debt in a Growing Business
item.description: Tech debt isn't just a developer problem. It's a leadership and growth problem. Here's how it actually slows businesses down — and what to do about it.

item.title: OKRs Without the Bureaucracy
item.description: OKRs work. The way most companies implement them doesn't. Here's how to get the alignment and focus OKRs are designed to create without the overhead that kills momentum.

item.title: What Data Governance Actually Means in Practice
item.description: Data governance sounds like compliance theatre. Done properly, it's how organisations make data usable, trustworthy, and safe at scale. Here's what it actually involves.

item.title: The Case for a Chief AI Officer — and When Not to Hire One
item.description: The Chief AI Officer role is becoming a fixture in leadership teams. But it's the right answer to a specific set of organisational problems — not a universal solution. Here's how to think about it.

item.title: Why Technology Projects Fail at Handover — and How to Fix It
item.description: Most technology projects succeed in build and fail in handover. The gap between what the development team built and what the operating team receives is where value is lost.

item.title: How to Structure Your First 90 Days as a Non-Executive Director
item.description: The first 90 days as a NED are the most important — and the least supported. Here's how to use the time well, build the relationships that matter, and establish credibility before you start contributing.

item.title: What Good Looks Like for an AI-Ready Board
item.description: Most boards are unprepared for the AI decisions now landing on their agendas. Here's what an AI-ready board actually looks like — and what it takes to build one.

item.title: The Difference Between a KPI and a Metric — and Why It Matters
item.description: Most organisations are drowning in metrics while being blind to performance. Here's the difference between a KPI and a metric — and how to find the numbers that actually tell you something.

item.title: How to Write an AI Business Case That Actually Gets Approved
item.description: Most AI business cases are rejected not because the AI doesn't work, but because the case is made in the wrong language for the wrong audience. Here's how to write one that gets approved.

item.title: How to run a board meeting that actually matters
item.description: Most board meetings are status updates with governance theatre. Here's how to structure a board meeting where the right decisions get made by the right people.

item.title: What digital-first operations actually means
item.description: Digital-first operations isn't about having more software. It's about designing processes where the digital layer drives the outcome, not just records it.

item.title: Why most companies automate the wrong things first
item.description: Most companies start automation with the visible, easy processes — and leave the high-value decisions untouched. Here's how to identify what's actually worth automating.

item.title: The difference between a roadmap and a plan
item.description: A roadmap tells you where you're going. A plan tells you how to get there. Most teams have one and call it the other — and it costs them dearly.

item.title: How to hire a product manager who actually builds
item.description: Most PM hiring processes select for people who present well, not people who ship. Here's how to tell the difference — and what to look for in a PM who actually builds.

item.title: What I learned from 20 years of bad strategy decks
item.description: After two decades of seeing strategy decks that don't work, I've noticed the same sins keep appearing. Here's what bad strategy looks like — and what good looks like instead.

item.title: Why your CTO and CMO don't speak the same language
item.description: CTOs think in systems. CMOs think in stories. The gap between them is where roadmaps stall, data gets misread, and AI projects die in committee.

item.title: The ops stack every digital business needs — and most don't have
item.description: Most digital businesses run on 15 disconnected tools and call it an ops stack. Here's what a real connected operations stack looks like — and why it matters.

item.title: What makes a great product brief — and why most teams skip it
item.description: A product brief isn't a spec doc. It's a contract between what the business needs and what the team is building. Most teams skip it and pay for it later.

item.title: How to think about AI ROI — and why most frameworks get it wrong
item.description: Most AI ROI frameworks measure the wrong things. Here's a better way to think about value that actually holds up inside a real business.

item.title: What Is Agentic AI — and What Does It Mean for Non-Technical Business Leaders?
item.description: Agentic AI systems can plan, make decisions, and take actions autonomously. For business leaders, this changes what's possible — and what's at stake. Here's a clear-headed introduction.

item.title: What Boards Need to Know About Shadow AI Right Now
item.description: Shadow AI — employees using AI tools without corporate knowledge or approval — is already inside your organisation. Here's what boards need to understand and what to do about it.

item.title: Three Digital Transformations I've Seen Fail — and What Actually Went Wrong
item.description: Most digital transformation failures share the same underlying causes. Drawing on direct experience, here are three patterns I've watched destroy value — and what a different outcome would have required.

item.title: Digitisation vs Digitalisation vs Digital Transformation: The Difference Actually Matters
item.description: Digitisation, digitalisation, and digital transformation are not the same thing. Conflating them leads to misaligned strategy and wasted investment. Here's the clear distinction.

item.title: What Is a Fractional Leader — and When Does It Make More Sense Than a Full Hire?
item.description: Fractional leadership is growing fast — but it's not right for every situation. Here's when a fractional CMO, CFO, or CTO genuinely makes sense, and when it doesn't.

item.title: What Is a Non-Executive Director — and Does Your Business Need One?
item.description: What is a Non-Executive Director, what do they actually do, and when does your business genuinely need one? A practical guide from someone who's held both sides of the table.

item.title: What Is AI Fluency — and Why Every Executive Needs It in 2026
item.description: AI fluency isn't about learning to code. It's about knowing enough to lead well in a world where AI is reshaping every business decision. Here's what it actually means.

item.title: Board Advisor vs Non-Executive Director: What's the Actual Difference?
item.description: Board advisors and Non-Executive Directors are often used interchangeably — but the differences in legal responsibility, time commitment, and compensation are significant. Here's how to tell them apart.

item.title: How to Land Your First Board Advisor Role
item.description: Most people wait to be discovered. That's why they wait forever. Here's the practical approach to landing your first board advisor role — from positioning to outreach.

item.title: What is a Digital Strategy — and Why Most Companies Don't Actually Have One
item.description: Most organisations think they have a digital strategy. What they actually have is a list of technology projects. Here's what a real digital strategy looks like — and how to build one.
